articles.title: Watch Artemis 2 race back to Earth in this telescope livestream tonight
articles.description: A Virtual Telescope Project livestream will track NASA's Artemis 2 Orion spacecraft as it races back toward Earth. Here's how to watch it live.

articles.title: Artemis II Flight Day 9: Crew Prepares to Come Home
articles.description: On their last full day in space, the Artemis II crew began the morning with “Lonesome Drifter” by Charley Crockett as they approached Earth at 147,337 miles.

articles.title: Artemis 2 astronaut poses for epic selfie | Space photo of the day for April 9, 2026
articles.description: Astronaut Christina Koch poses with zero-gravity indicator "Rise" in the viewport of the Artemis 2 Orion spacecraft.

articles.title: What returning to lunar space means for human health
articles.description: Preparing to send astronauts back to the moon is no small feat. Spaceflight exposes the body to both known and unknown risks, some of which are subtle, cumulative, and difficult to predict. When humans were last on the lunar surface in 1972, they stayed for just over three days. On that final missio

articles.title: 'We all pretty much broke down right there': Inside the Artemis 2 astronauts' emotional moment near the moon
articles.description: Just after they broke the human spaceflight distance record, the Artemis 2 astronauts shared a powerful moment that deepened their already profound bond.

articles.title: Artemis 2 mission sends 'Kerbal Space Program' player numbers soaring to the Mun
articles.description: Anything NASA can do, you can do too.

articles.title: All eyes on Orion’s heat shield: Artemis 2 astronauts will hit Earth's atmosphere at nearly 24,000 mph on April 10
articles.description: Artemis 2's Orion capsule will hit Earth's atmosphere at nearly 24,000 mph on April 10. A heat shield and 11 parachutes will help it survive the fiery trip and splash down safely.

articles.title: Twin NASA Control Rooms Support Artemis Safety, Success
articles.description: Twin control rooms at NASA’s Marshall Space Flight Center in Huntsville, Alabama, are actively supporting real-time mission operations in lunar orbit as part of the agency’s Artemis II mission, helping ensure astronaut safety and mission success as the crew prepares to return to Earth Friday, April

articles.title: Solar Eclipse of the Heart
articles.description: The Moon, seen here backlit by the Sun during a solar eclipse on April 6, 2026, is photographed by one of the cameras on the Orion spacecraft’s solar array wings. During lunar flyby, the Artemis II astronauts observed a solar eclipse that only they could see due to their unique position. See more ph

articles.title: 'Audible screams of delight' from NASA scientists over micrometeorite impacts on the moon witnessed by Artemis 2 astronauts
articles.description: NASA scientists are thrilled with the Artemis 2 astronauts' moon flyby observations —especially the micrometeor impact flashes they saw.

articles.title: Artemis II Flight Day 8: Crew Conducts Key Tests on Return to Earth
articles.description: Editor’s Note: This blog was updated on April 8, 2026, to update the time for the crew news conference. articles.title: Artemis 2 astronauts spy the Milky Way | Space photo of the day for April 8, 2026
articles.description: The image captures the glowing ribbon of the galactic plane, where the vast majority of the Milky Way's stars are concentrated.

articles.title: A lunar base or a lunar economy?
articles.description: We applaud the lunar base vision laid out by NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman on March 24. Placing this stake in the lunar regolith is long overdue. What is missing from the mission architecture, however, is a vision for long-term economic development on the moon. It will be a profound missed oppor

articles.title: Artemis 2 moon astronauts capture space history — on their iPhones
articles.description: The Artemis 2 astronauts are capturing spectacular photos of the moon and Earth on their iPhones in space.

articles.title: Artemis 2 science gets underway as Orion begins its return trip
articles.description: As Artemis 2 begins its return to Earth, scientists are just starting to review the images and observations taken by the crew as they flew around the moon. The post Artemis 2 science gets underway as Orion begins its return trip appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: 'It just made it even more special': Being so far from Earth makes you appreciate our planet even more, Artemis 2 astronaut says
articles.description: Seeing Earth from space changes your perspective. And this "overview effect" is magnified when you're looking back from beyond the moon, according to Artemis 2's Christina Koch.

articles.title: Artemis II Flight Day 7: First Return Correction Burn Complete
articles.description: At 8:03 p.m. EDT, the Orion spacecraft, named Integrity, ignited its thrusters for 15 seconds, producing a change in velocity of 1.6 feet-per-second and guiding the Artemis II crew toward Earth. NASA astronaut Christina Koch and CSA (C

articles.title: The Artemis 2 space toilet is actually working fine. But there is another problem
articles.description: "The challenge that we're working through is evacuating the tank."

articles.title: NASA’s Artemis II Crew Beams Official Moon Flyby Photos to Earth
articles.description: Editor’s note: Some photo captions were updated on April 8, 2026, to reflect ongoing scientific observations and discussion about the images. articles.title: Earthset
articles.description: The Artemis II crew captured this view of Earth setting on April 6, 2026, as they flew around the Moon. articles.title: Relive Artemis 2's epic moon flyby with these amazing photos
articles.description: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts chronicled their historic loop around the moon on Monday (April 7) in great detail. Take a look at their stunning images here.

articles.title: Experience the Rollout of SLS Hardware for Artemis III
articles.description: Digital creators and social media users are invited to register to attend the rollout of the third SLS (Space Launch System) core stage as it is prepped to head from NASA’s Michoud Assembly Facility to NASA’s Kennedy Space Center. articles.title: Artemis 2 captures historic 'Earthset' photo | Space photo of the day for April 7, 2026
articles.description: Artemis 2 astronauts saw Earth slip from view during their lunar flyby on Flight Day 6.

articles.title: NASA to Host Media Call with Artemis II Crew on Way Home from Moon
articles.description: Editor’s note: This advisory was updated Wednesday, April 8, 2026, to reflect a time change in the event. articles.title: Artemis II Flight Day 7: Crew Makes Long‑Distance Call, Begins Return
articles.description: After their historic lunar flyby on April 6, the Artemis II crew is awake and preparing for the journey back to Earth. The crew started the day 36,286 miles from the Moon and 236,022 miles from Earth, waking to the sounds of “Tokyo Drifting,” by Glass Animals and Denzel Curry. At 1:23 p.m. EDT, the

articles.title: 'Unreal' solar eclipse: Artemis 2 crew just saw one of the rarest sights in spaceflight history
articles.description: Artemis 2 astronauts witness a rare solar eclipse from beyond the moon during their historic lunar flyby.

articles.title: The Artemis 2 astronauts just flew by the moon. Lunar scientists can't wait for what's next
articles.description: "Apollo happened before I was born, and the idea that we will see humans on the moon within our lifetimes, with decades of advances in science and technology behind them, is remarkable."

articles.title: Moon milestones: A rundown of Artemis 2's many spaceflight firsts
articles.description: You've probably heard it by now: NASA’s Artemis 2 mission was the first to launch astronauts to fly by the moon in more than 50 years. But that was just the first of the firsts for this mission.

articles.title: 'This is really big stuff.' Trump hails NASA Artemis 2 astronauts after historic moon flyby feat
articles.description: President Donald Trump on Monday night (April 6) hailed the courage and grit of NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts after their historic flyby around the moon.

articles.title: Artemis II Flight Day 6: Crew Wraps Historic Lunar Flyby
articles.description: The Artemis II mission wrapped up a historic seven-hour lunar flyby, marking humanity’s first return to the Moon since Apollo 17 in 1972 and capturing images of the lunar far side. After the lunar observation period concluded, the Artemis II crew was congratulated by President Donald J. Trump, in a

articles.title: Artemis II breaks record, conducts lunar flyby
articles.description: Orion spacecraft Integrity, carrying astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen, successfully… The post Artemis II breaks record, conducts lunar flyby appeared first on NASASpaceFlight.com.

articles.title: Artemis 2 astronauts fly around the moon in record-breaking lunar loop by NASA
articles.description: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts viewed parts of the moon never before seen with human eyes during their epic lunar flyby today (April 6) —and set a spaceflight record in the process.

articles.title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to loft Cygnus to ISS, Minotaur IV to launch from California

articles.title: Moon music: Here's a Spotify playlist of the Artemis 2 crew's wakeup songs
articles.description: Artemis 2 wake-up songs are mission control's way of starting the crew's day off with positivity. Here's the Spotify playlist.

articles.title: Moon memorial: Artemis 2 astronauts name lunar 'bright spot' after mission commander's late wife
articles.description: As they broke the record for farthest distance from Earth ever traveled by an astronaut, NASA's Artemis 2 crew took a moment to dedicate a few features on the moon's surface for their spacecraft and lost loved ones.

articles.title: Artemis 2 breaks humanity's all-time distance record during historic loop around the moon (video)
articles.description: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts have now traveled farther from Earth than any people in history, breaking the mark set by the Apollo 13 crew in April 1970.

articles.title: Artemis II Flight Day 6: Lunar Flyby Updates
articles.description: NASA’s live coverage of the Artemis II lunar flyby is underway on NASA+, Amazon Prime, Apple TV, Hulu, Netflix, HBO Max, and Roku, alongside the agency’s 24/7 coverage on its YouTube channel. Learn how to stream NASA content through a variety of online platforms, including social media.

articles.title: The moon up close: How the Artemis 2 astronauts are photographing their historic lunar flyby
articles.description: The Artemis 2 astronauts might be the first humans to survey certain areas of the moon's far side, and they're chronicling their historic journey in detail.

articles.title: Artemis II Flight Day 6: Crew Ready for Lunar Flyby
articles.description: The Artemis II crew is awake and beginning preparations for today’s lunar flyby — a first for humans since Apollo 17 in 1972. The sounds of “Good Morning,” by Mandisa and TobyMac played as NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, and Christina Koch, along with CSA (Canadian Space Agency) astrona

articles.title: Astronaut Victor Glover is the latest in a long line of Black American explorers − including York, the enslaved man who played a key role in the Lewis and Clark expedition
articles.description: Glover's achievement is worth celebrating. But it's also worth remembering that he belongs to a long and underappreciated history.

articles.title: NASA Artemis 2 astronauts to make historic moon flyby today. Here's what to expect hour by hour (timeline)
articles.description: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts will make a historic moon flyby today (April 6). Here's how it will happen, hour by hour.

articles.title: Humanity is back at the moon! Artemis 2 astronauts arrive in lunar space
articles.description: The four astronauts of NASA's Artemis 2 mission have arrived at the moon. They entered the lunar sphere of influence early Monday morning (April 6).

articles.title: Artemis II Flight Day 5: Correction Burn Complete
articles.description: Mission control teams in Houston and the Artemis II crew completed an outbound correction burn to refine the Orion spacecraft’s trajectory to the Moon. The burn began at 11:03 p.m. EDT and lasted 17.5 seconds. NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, and Christ

articles.title: When the moon hits your eye from your Orion ship up high, that's a 'mare' for Artemis 2
articles.description: The four Artemis 2 astronauts had a chance to observe the moon this weekend, and to say they were thrilled may be an understatement of cosmic proportions.

articles.title: Artemis II Flight Day 5: Crew Demos Suits, Readies for Lunar Flyby
articles.description: The Artemis II crew of NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and CSA (Canadian Space Agency) astronaut Jeremy Hansen, continue their workday aboard the Orion spacecraft.

articles.title: Artemis 2 astronauts are about to see one of the rarest skywatching sights of all — a solar eclipse from beyond the moon
articles.description: The four Artemis 2 astronauts will see a solar eclipse from beyond the moon's far side on Monday evening (April 6), and they'll use the opportunity to make some science observations.

articles.title: The breakout star of NASA's Artemis 2 moon mission isn't an astronaut — it's the space toilet
articles.description: The on-again, off-again lunar loo allows astronauts to "go" at the moon like no one's gone before.

articles.title: Artemis II Flight Day 5: Crew Starts Day with Suit Demo
articles.description: The morning opened to the beat of “Working Class Heroes (Work)” by CeeLo Green as the Artemis II crew, now flying about 65,235 miles from the Moon, began preparations for their first test objective: an evaluation of the Orion Crew Survival System suit. The crew also heard a special message from Apol

articles.title: Artemis 2 moon astronauts will try to recreate Apollo 8's historic 'Earthrise' photo during April 6 flyby
articles.description: Plans are in place for the crew of Artemis 2 to try to replicate one of the most famous images ever taken from space — Apollo 8's shot of Earth rising over the moon's horizon.

articles.title: This is the inspiring Easter message an Artemis 2 astronaut has for Earth while traveling to the moon (video)
articles.description: NASA astronaut Victor Glover delivered an impromptu Easter message as his Artemis 2 Orion capsule sped toward the moon on Saturday (April 4).
